questions.q: What was Sumantra's role in King Dasharatha's court?
questions.explanation: Sumantra served Dasharatha as a trusted minister and as his personal charioteer.

questions.q: Sumantra was the charioteer of which king of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Sumantra was the faithful charioteer of King Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

questions.q: In which kingdom did Sumantra serve as a minister?
questions.explanation: Sumantra served the royal court of Kosala, whose capital was Ayodhya.

questions.q: Whom did Sumantra drive in his chariot to the edge of the forest at the start of the exile?
questions.explanation: Sumantra drove Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana in his chariot toward the forest as they left for exile.

questions.q: What emotion is most associated with Sumantra when he returned to Ayodhya after leaving Rama?
questions.explanation: Sumantra returned to a grieving Ayodhya heartbroken at having to leave Rama in the forest.

questions.q: Sumantra is best remembered for which quality in his service?
questions.explanation: Sumantra is celebrated as the faithful and devoted servant of the royal house of Ayodhya.

questions.q: What vehicle did Sumantra use to carry Rama out of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Sumantra carried Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana out of the city in his royal chariot.

questions.q: How is Sumantra generally regarded by the people of Ayodhya?
options: As an enemy of the king
As a trusted and beloved counselor
As a rival to the throne
questions.explanation: Sumantra was a trusted and respected minister, dear to both the king and the people.

questions.q: Whose exile order did Sumantra have to help carry out, however unwillingly?
options: Sita's exile in the Uttara Kanda
questions.explanation: Sumantra reluctantly assisted with Rama's departure into exile by driving his chariot.

questions.q: After leaving Rama at the forest's edge, where did Sumantra go?
options: He stayed in the forest with Rama
questions.explanation: Sumantra returned alone to Ayodhya, bringing the sorrowful news of Rama's departure.

questions.q: When Rama set out for exile, why did Sumantra accompany him in the chariot?
options: To spy on Rama for Kaikeyi
On Dasharatha's order, to drive Rama as far as possible
To abandon him in the forest
Because Rama forced him to come
questions.explanation: Dasharatha sent his trusted charioteer Sumantra to drive Rama, hoping to ease his beloved son's journey.

questions.q: On the banks of which river did Sumantra finally have to part from Rama?
questions.explanation: Sumantra parted from Rama near the Ganga, where Guha the boatman ferried Rama across.

questions.q: Which Nishada chieftain did Sumantra encounter when Rama reached the Ganga?
questions.explanation: At the Ganga, Rama met Guha the Nishada chief, and it was there that Sumantra turned back.

questions.q: What did Rama ask Sumantra to tell Dasharatha and the mothers upon his return?
options: That Rama had renounced them forever
Messages of reassurance and that they should not grieve
questions.explanation: Rama sent words of comfort through Sumantra, urging his family not to grieve over his exile.

questions.q: How did Sumantra react when Rama insisted he turn the chariot back to Ayodhya?
options: He happily agreed and left at once
He was overcome with sorrow and reluctant to leave Rama
He refused and stayed with Rama
questions.explanation: Sumantra was grief-stricken and pleaded to remain, only reluctantly returning at Rama's insistence.

questions.q: When Sumantra brought back the empty chariot, what was the mood in Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: The whole of Ayodhya was plunged into mourning at the sight of Sumantra returning without Rama.

questions.q: Which queen's demand set in motion the events that forced Sumantra to drive Rama away?
questions.explanation: Queen Kaikeyi's boons demanding Rama's exile and Bharata's coronation set these events in motion.

questions.q: How did the horses of Sumantra's chariot reportedly behave as he was forced to leave Rama?
options: They were reluctant and shed tears, unwilling to move
They refused to be harnessed at all
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that even the chariot horses wept and moved unwillingly as they bore Sumantra away from Rama.

questions.q: Within which book (Kanda) of the Ramayana does Sumantra's role in the exile mainly fall?
questions.explanation: Sumantra's part in Rama's exile is told in the Ayodhya Kanda, the book of Ayodhya.

questions.q: When Sumantra returned, to whom did he have to give the painful report of Rama's departure?
questions.explanation: Sumantra reported the sorrowful news of Rama's parting to the grieving Dasharatha and the queens of Ayodhya.

questions.q: What best describes the relationship of trust between Dasharatha and Sumantra?
options: A bond of deep confidence and friendship
questions.explanation: Sumantra was among Dasharatha's most trusted ministers, sharing a bond of deep confidence with the king.

questions.q: Before Rama's exile, Sumantra also served as a messenger and counselor during which earlier royal matter?
options: The summoning of sages and ministers for state counsel
questions.explanation: As a senior minister, Sumantra carried the king's word and helped convene sages and councilors on state matters.

questions.q: Sumantra is said to have helped summon which sage to advise the childless Dasharatha about a sacrifice for sons?
questions.explanation: Sumantra recounted the story of the sage Rishyasringa, who was brought to perform the rites that gave Dasharatha sons.

questions.q: Which two great sacrifices, leading to the birth of Dasharatha's sons, are connected with the counsel Sumantra conveyed?
questions.explanation: Sumantra's counsel pointed toward the Ashvamedha and Putrakameshti sacrifices performed to obtain sons for Dasharatha.

questions.q: When Sumantra reported back, Dasharatha was tormented by the memory of an old curse for accidentally killing whom?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha recalled killing the young ascetic Shravana Kumara by mistake, whose parents' curse foretold his death from grief over a son.

questions.q: After Rama's departure, what soon befell King Dasharatha, deepening Ayodhya's sorrow that Sumantra witnessed?
options: He abdicated and went to the forest
He died of grief over Rama's exile
He recovered and ruled long after
questions.explanation: Dasharatha died of grief soon after Rama's exile, a sorrow Sumantra and all Ayodhya shared.

questions.q: When Bharata returned and set out to bring Rama back, what role did the experienced Sumantra play?
options: He accompanied and guided the party toward Rama
He stayed behind to rule Ayodhya
He had already retired from court
questions.explanation: As a senior minister, Sumantra accompanied Bharata's party that journeyed to the forest to seek Rama's return.

questions.q: Which other senior minister of Dasharatha is most often named alongside Sumantra in the royal council?
questions.explanation: Sumantra is named among Dasharatha's eight ministers, listed with figures such as Vijaya and Jayanta in the council.

questions.q: What is the literal sense of a name like 'Sumantra,' fitting his role as a wise counselor?
options: One who is a great warrior
One of good counsel or good counsel itself
questions.explanation: The name Sumantra carries the sense of 'good counsel,' suiting his role as the king's wise and trusted minister.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, how does Sumantra try to comfort the grieving Kaushalya after his return?
options: By telling her Rama has died
By conveying Rama's reassuring words and praising his steadfastness
By blaming her for the exile
By refusing to speak to her
questions.explanation: Sumantra consoled Kaushalya by relating Rama's loving, reassuring messages and his calm resolve in accepting exile.
